zh.lifeSummary: 當Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby於1201年出生在威爾士布洛可爾布洛可時，她的父親Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall55歲，母親Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare30歲。她於1219年5月14日之前與Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby在威爾士布洛可爾布洛可結婚。他們至少是7個女兒的父母。她去世於1245年4月27日在英國德比夏爾阿希布恩享年44歲，埋葬在威爾士蒙姆爾特特 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby于1201年出生在威尔士布洛可爾布洛可时，她的父亲Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall55岁，母亲Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare30岁。她于1219年5月14日之前与Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby在威尔士布洛可爾布洛可结婚。他们至少是7个女儿的父母。她去世于1245年4月27日在英国德比夏爾阿希布恩享年44岁，埋葬在威尔士蒙姆爾特特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Sibyl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Sybil .  Even in classical times there was confusion between the two vowels in this word.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby was born in 1201, in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, her father, Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall, was 55 and her mother, Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare, was 30. She married Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby before 14 May 1219, in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 7 daughters. She died on 27 April 1245, in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Wales.

fr.Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales: Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby est née en 1201, à Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles, son père, Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall, avait 55 ans et sa mère, Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby avant 14 mai 1219, à Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 avril 1245, à Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby 1201 in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall 55 und ihre Mutter Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare 30. Sie hat vor 14. Mai 1219 in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 27. April 1245 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England und wurde in Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby è nata il nel 1201 nel Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles, sua padre, Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall, aveva 55 anni e sua madre, Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby prima del 14 maggio 1219, nel Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figlie. Morì il 27 aprile 1245, nel Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derbyが1201年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアペンブロクで生まれた時，父Earl of Pembroke William I Marshallは55歳，母Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clareは30歳であった。1219年5月14日以前にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアペンブロクでSir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1245年4月27日にイギリスデルブイシュアイアアシュボウルンで44歳で死去し，ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアティンテルンティンテルン アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby(이)가 1201년 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall(은)는 55세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1219년 5월 14일 이전 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1245년 4월 27일 영국 더비실르 애쉬보우른에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 몬모우스실르 틴터 틴터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby nasceu em 1201, em Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, seu pai, Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall, tinha 55 anos e sua mãe, Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby antes 14 de maio de 1219, em Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de abril de 1245, em Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales.

ro.Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales: Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Țara Galilor, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby родилась в 1201 г. в местности пемброк, пемброкешир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby перед 14 май 1219 г. в местности пемброк, пемброкешир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 7 дочерей. Она умерла 27 апреля 1245 г. в местности ашбурн, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности тинтирн эбби, тинтирн, монмоутшир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby nació en 1201, en Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, su papá, Earl of Pembroke William I Marshall, tenía 55 años y su mamá, Countess of Pembrok Isabel Fitzgilbert de Clare, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby antes de 14 de mayo de 1219, en Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijas. Murió el 27 de abril de 1245, en Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultada en Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales.
